description: Part 7 of Flashcards Set 2 (Policy provisions, group & health insurance basics)

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an unfair claim settlement practice?
options: Failing to acknowledge and act promptly with respect to an insurance claim
Compelling an insured to initiate a lawsuit by offering less on an insurance claim
Failing to accept or deny a claim within reasonable time after proof of loss is submitted
Needing written documentation of claim details
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Identify what the question is asking (rule, calculation, or outcome).
Step 2: Apply the LLQP rule or formula that governs the scenario.
Step 3: Eliminate distractors using the facts and remove options that conflict with the scenario.
Step 4: Select the best answer. Option D: Needing written documentation of claim details.
optionFeedback: "Failing to acknowledge and act promptly with respect to an insurance claim" is not the exception here. It is one of the items that is normally true or required. The correct answer is "Needing written documentation of claim details" because that statement is the one that matches the rule being tested.

"Compelling an insured to initiate a lawsuit by offering less on an insurance claim" is not the exception here. It is one of the items that is normally true or required. The correct answer is "Needing written documentation of claim details" because that statement is the one that matches the rule being tested.

"Failing to accept or deny a claim within reasonable time after proof of loss is submitted" is not the exception here. It is one of the items that is normally true or required. The correct answer is "Needing written documentation of claim details" because that statement is the one that matches the rule being tested.
Needing written documentation of claim details.

questions.question: Which of the following is considered an accurate statement of an unfair trade practice?
options: Coercion involves making a malicious statement about the financial condition of an insurance company
Twisting involves an agent using misrepresentation to convince a policyowner to cancel their current policy so that they can purchase a new life insurance policy with another company
No more than 25% of an agent's insurance sales are allowed to come from controlled business
Rebating occurs when someone intentionally deceives another with the intent to gain financially
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Identify what the question is asking (rule, calculation, or outcome).
Step 2: Apply the LLQP rule or formula that governs the scenario.
Step 3: Eliminate distractors using the facts and remove options that conflict with the scenario.
Step 4: Select the best answer. Option B: Twisting involves an agent using misrepresentation to convince a policyowner to cancel their current policy so that they can purchase a new life insurance policy with another company.
optionFeedback: "Coercion involves making a malicious statement about the financial condition of an insurance..." is a different statement than the one this question is testing. The correct answer is "Twisting involves an agent using misrepresentation to convince a policyowner to cancel their current policy so that they can purchase a new life insurance policy with another company" because that statement is the one that matches the rule being tested.
Twisting involves an agent using misrepresentation to convince a policyowner to cancel their current policy so that they can purchase a new life insurance policy with another company.

"No more than 25% of an agent's insurance sales are allowed to come from controlled business" is a different statement than the one this question is testing. The correct answer is "Twisting involves an agent using misrepresentation to convince a policyowner to cancel their current policy so that they can purchase a new life insurance policy with another company" because that statement is the one that matches the rule being tested.

"Rebating occurs when someone intentionally deceives another with the intent to gain financially" is a different statement than the one this question is testing. The correct answer is "Twisting involves an agent using misrepresentation to convince a policyowner to cancel their current policy so that they can purchase a new life insurance policy with another company" because that statement is the one that matches the rule being tested.

"copy of the agent's insurance license" is a different statement than the one this question is testing. The correct answer is "Notice to existing and replacing insurers of intention to replace" because that statement is the one that matches the rule being tested.
